title: Mahasar Taal Trek
highlights: Completely Guided trek
Moderate diffculty trek
All logistic support available

detail.snapshot: Hidden deep in the Garhwal Himalayas, Mahasar Tal is not just a destination—it’s a feeling. A sacred alpine lake, believed to be connected to the Pandavas and protected by a powerful Nag Devta presence, this untouched trail offers something rare—stillness, solitude, and raw connection. No crowds. No noise. Just you, the mountains, and the journey within. Alpine Ops Originals – Elite Guided Treks Small teams. Real experiences. Mountains as they are meant to be.
detail.footerNote: Places are limited and filled on a first-come basis.

stay.title: Stay & Accommodation
highlights: Shared tents at campsite

days.heading: Arrival – Boodha Kedar
items: Arrival at the serene Himalayan village of Boodha Kedar
Settle in and soak in the calm, spiritual atmosphere
Evening briefing & overnight stay

days.heading: Boodha Kedar to Mahasar Tal (9 km)
items: Moderate uphill trek through dense forests and remote mountain trails
Gradual transition into untouched alpine terrain
Reach the sacred Mahasar Tal—still, powerful, and deeply calming
Spend time by the lake, absorbing its silence and mythological aura
Overnight in tents under open skies

days.heading: Descent & Dispersal
items: Descend back through forests and ridgelines
Trek concludes at Boodha Kedar
Dispersal with memories that stay longer than the journey

whoCanJoin: Immersive nature experience—not just a hike
Designed for those seeking clarity, stillness, and connection

title: Mt Kanamo Expedition
highlights: Trekking peak of 5900M
Operate like a small team
Disciplined, Efficient and Minimalistic

detail.snapshot: Ready to break the 19,000 ft barrier? Kanamo peak is the ultimate high altitude challenge. No technical gear, just pure Grit, Thin air and best views in Spiti.

title: Mt CCKN Expedition
highlights: Technical peak of 6300M
World class climbing equipments and safety systems
Small focussed team for serious climbers
A Genuine Himalayan expedition environment

detail.snapshot: Rising above the stark cold desert of Spiti, Mount Chau Chau Kang Nilda (6300m) is one of the most striking peaks dominating the skyline near Kaza. The mountain was first climbed in 1939 by a British expedition led by Major J.O.M. Roberts, and since then it has attracted mountaineers from across the world seeking a serious yet achievable Himalayan expedition peak. CCKN is known for its long glacier approaches, crevassed terrain, and steep snow slopes near the summit ridge, demanding endurance, sound mountain judgement, and the ability to move efficiently at high altitude. The climb is a true expedition experience, not a simple trekking objective. From 25 June to 06 July, Alpine Ops will attempt this iconic Spitian summit in a tightly managed expedition starting and ending in Kaza.

title: Spiti Ice Climbing workshop
highlights: Learn to climb frozen waterfalls from ex-military experts.
Master ice climbing in Spiti with India's best instructors.
Professional training, world-class gear, and total safety.
The ultimate winter ice climbing workshop in the Himalayas.

detail.snapshot: join this workshop in Spiti for a week of adventure across the frozen waterfalls of Tabo and Mane. Designed for both beginner and intermediate climbers, the program features hands-on training from ex-military instructors with years of cold-weather expertise, using international-standard gear and strict safety protocols. Beyond the climb, you’ll stay in traditional local homestays, enjoy authentic Spiti cuisine, and explore the region's unique culture and landscapes. It is the perfect blend of technical skill-building and a deep, adventurous dive into the heart of the Himalayas in winter.
detail.footerNote: Places are limited and filled on a first-come basis.
